findings.description: The NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources logo/header graphic appears differently styled in the HTML version but is present.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC tree logo with 'Energy, Mineral & Land Resources' and 'ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' text beneath it. HTML shows a similar but slightly different rendering of the same logo area.

findings.description: Page 2 in the HTML contains a duplicate rendering of the memorandum content that already appeared on page 1, along with a redundant letterhead/footer section splitting the content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 is blank except for a header/footer area. HTML page 2 shows a full re-rendering of the memorandum header, To/From/Subject fields, first paragraph, and outline items III and IV, duplicating content from page 1.

findings.description: The HTML page 2 duplicates content from page 1, creating a confusing reading order where a reader encounters the same memorandum information twice.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 is essentially blank/transition. HTML page 2 repeats the MEMORANDUM heading, date, To/From/Subject lines, opening paragraph, and partial outline.

findings.description: Page 4 in the HTML shows a duplicate/split rendering with a letterhead footer breaking mid-content, then continuing with duplicated content from the oral comments section.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 is a clean continuation of page 3 content. HTML page 4 shows the outline items III and IV at the top, then a full letterhead/footer break, then re-starts with History/Background and begins Section II content, duplicating earlier material.

findings.description: HTML pages 5-6 contain duplicate renderings of the oral and written comment summaries with letterhead breaks splitting the content, creating a confusing double-reading of the same material.
findings.evidence: PDF pages show clean continuous content. HTML inserts letterhead/footer sections mid-content and re-renders the bullet point summaries, causing duplication of Environmental, Property Values, Health Issues, etc. bullet points.

findings.description: HTML page 8 duplicates the Written Public Comment Summary and Attachments sections that were already rendered in previous pages.
findings.evidence: PDF shows clean page flow. HTML re-renders the Written Public Comment Summary with Environmental, Health Issues, Criticism of Administration, etc. bullet points, followed by a duplicate Attachments section.

findings.description: HTML page 10 contains yet another duplicate rendering of the Attachments section (IV. Attachments) with its six sub-items.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 shows the Attachments section once. HTML renders it again on page 10 after already showing it on page 8.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements, which is semantically incorrect but does not affect content fidelity. The heading hierarchy is reasonable: h1 for MEMORANDUM, h2 for each major section (I, II, III, IV).
findings.evidence: Two <main> tags are present in the HTML. Heading hierarchy itself is acceptable.

findings.description: Most images have empty or generic alt text. The first image has a reasonable alt text describing the NC DEQ logo, but subsequent images have empty alt attributes.
findings.evidence: First img: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo'. Remaining images: alt=''. These appear to be page header/footer decorative images, so empty alt is acceptable.

findings.description: The page footer text (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | ...) is repeated multiple times throughout the HTML, matching the PDF's per-page footer. This is not incorrect per se, as it mirrors the source, but is duplicated content from the PDF's page formatting.
findings.evidence: Footer block 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mineral and Land Resources...' appears 5 times in the HTML.
